If you source from China, you need to hear this. Real talk. Your sourcing budget is leaking 4% a year and you're looking in the wrong place to plug it. Three things are happening every wire. One. Your bank quotes you a USD-CNY rate. You accept it. That rate is 4% worse than the live market rate. Two. The 4% gets baked into the conversion. No fee line, no statement, nothing to alert you. Three. On 500K of annual sourcing, that's 20,000 dollars that left your business and never reached your supplier. You can negotiate harder with the factory all day. The 4% is not on the supplier side. It's on the bank side. WorldFirst runs the same wires at 0.3 to 0.5%. The leak shrinks from 20K to under 3K. You don'tcan't get the past years back. But youYou can stop year four right now. Search WorldFirst. Period.

Skeptic test · what a Malaysian SMB might push back on

The three-things-happening structure is clean and educational. 'Looking in the wrong place to plug it' frames the misdiagnosis well. 'You can't get the past years back. You can stop year four right now' is a sharp close. The 4% rate-vs-mid-market description is technically accurate framing. Main red flag is the universal 4% and the assumption that 100% of the 20K leak gets eliminated, when WorldFirst still has a 0.3 to 0.5% margin. The math (20K to under 3K) is consistent but the close implies more recovery than is real.

  • Year four stop right now sound nice but you assume I switch all my wires. Got annual contract with my bank.
  • 4% worse than live market rate? Where you get the live market rate from. Reuters?
  • Under 3K leak. Means WorldFirst still got margin. Just smaller. Fine but say it lah.
  • Three things every wire is a clean breakdown. I like this one.
  • Who is WorldFirst? Sound American. Got SEA support?
#6 PG2 Gary Vee Opinion-led 112 words

Opinion-as-truth + compound math

Soc4.3 Aud4.1 Sales4.2 Overall4.2 HeatMedium

'Your bank is the most expensive part of your supply chain' is the sharpest single line in this batch. Tight at 97 words.

Pure original

I'll say it. The most expensive vendor in your business is the bank that processes your supplier wires and you've never thought about it that way. 500K of sourcing a year. 4% margin baked into the rate. 20K gone. Year two, your business grows, 22K gone. Year three, 24K. That's 66K over three years. That's a hire. That's a warehouse rack. That's your margin compression. And here's the part nobody talks about. You can't see it. It's not on the statement. It's not on the wire confirmation. It's baked into the rate your bank gave you on the conversion. WorldFirst runs at 0.3 to 0.5%. Same money, same suppliers, the 66K stays in your business. Period.

Brand-polish iteration deleted / added

I'll say it. The most expensive vendor in your business is the bank that processes your supplier wires and you've never thought about it that way. 500K of sourcing a year. 4% margin baked into the rate. 20K gone. Year two, your business grows, 22K gone. Year three, 24K. That's 66K over three years. That's a hire. That's a warehouse rack. That's your margin compression. And here's the part nobody talks about. You can't see it. It's not on the statement. It's not on the wire confirmation. It's baked into the rate your bank gave you on the conversion. WorldFirst runs at 0.3 to 0.5%. Same money, same suppliers, the 66K stays in your business. Period.

Skeptic test · what a Malaysian SMB might push back on

'Most expensive vendor is the bank' is an opinion claim with real teeth, framed as observation not fact. Compound growth math (66K over three years) carries same risk as PH2. 'That's a hire. That's a warehouse rack. That's your margin compression' is strong emotional ladder but doesn't overreach. 'You can't see it. It's not on the statement.' is largely true. Cleaner ending without 'Period' or call to action could be confusing but is intentional Gary Vee style. Strong overall, mid risk on the compound math.

  • Most expensive vendor is the bank. Damn, that hit. But got proof?
  • 10% growth assumption again. My business flat this year actually.
  • Where this 4% from? Different banks charge differently lah.
  • Hire, warehouse rack, ok inspiration but I want numbers comparison please.
  • No call to action at the end? Where I go to check WorldFirst?
#7 PH4 Alex Hormozi PAS 134 words

Mechanism-first reveal

Soc4.1 Aud4.1 Sales4.2 Overall4.1 HeatMedium

'The fee is not the fee' = sharp reframe. 'Banks make billions' is a broad claim that could draw pushback.

Pure original

Here's the problem most business owners miss when they source from China. You're negotiating with the wrong person. You spend three weeks pushing your supplier on unit price. You shave 2% off the PI. You feel great. Meanwhile, your bank is taking 4% of every wire through the exchange rate margin. On 500K of annual sourcing, that's 20,000 dollars. Twice what you saved negotiating. And it compounds. Year two, your budget grows 10%, the leak grows with it. 22K. Year three, 24K. Total three-year leak: 66K. The supplier never touched that money. It vanished into the conversion before the wire crossed the border. Stop. The supplier is not the leak. The rail is the leak. WorldFirst sits at 0.3 to 0.5%. Same wires, same suppliers, 17K of that 20K stays in your business. Period. Search WorldFirst. Period.

Brand-polish iteration deleted / added

Here's the problem most business owners miss when they source from China. You're negotiating with the wrong person. You spend three weeks pushing your supplier on unit price. You shave 2% off the PI. You feel great. Meanwhile, your bank is taking 4% of every wire through the exchange rate margin. On 500K of annual sourcing, that's 20,000 dollars. Twice what you saved negotiating. And it compounds. Year two, your budget grows 10%, the leak grows with it. 22K. Year three, 24K. Total three-year leak: 66K. The supplier never touched that money. It vanished into the conversion before the wire crossed the border. Stop. The supplier is not the leak. The rail is the leak. WorldFirst sits at 0.3 to 0.5%. Same wires, same suppliers, 17K of that 20K stays in your business. Period. Search WorldFirst. Period.

Skeptic test · what a Malaysian SMB might push back on

Cleanest mechanism explanation in the Hormozi set. The 'negotiating with the wrong person' reframe is psychologically strong and matches a real importer frustration. The 2% PI vs 4% bank comparison is illustrative and lands well. The 17K of 20K stays in business math (4% to 0.3%) is internally consistent. Main risk remains the universal 4% figure presented without qualifier. 'Vanished into the conversion before the wire crossed the border' is slightly poetic but defensible. Three-year 66K compound assumes constant 10% growth which is the same risk as PH2.

  • Wrong person to negotiate with, ok this point actually hit. Never thought of it that way.
  • 4% every wire? My bank not so bad. Where's the data from?
  • Compound assume 10% YoY growth. Lots of SMEs flat or down 2025.
  • 17K of 20K stays in business. So WorldFirst still take 3K. Got monthly fee?
  • Who is WorldFirst? Need to check if regulated here first.
#8 PG1 Gary Vee Opinion-led 118 words

Classic Stop / Real-talk opener

Soc4.0 Aud4.1 Sales4.2 Overall4.1 HeatMedium

Lost some of the original Gary Vee punch after polish. Still works but feels closer to baseline.

Pure original

Stop. Real talk for every business owner sourcing from China. Your sourcing budget shrinks 4% every year and you can't see it. 500K spend? Your bank quietly kept 20,000 dollars in the exchange rate margin. You'll never find it on a statement. It's baked into the conversion. And here's the part that gets me. You're negotiating MOQ with the supplier. You're squeezing unit price. You're chasing 1% on the PI. Meanwhile your bank is taking 4% of the whole budget. Wrong fight. Wrong opponent. WorldFirst runs the same wires at 0.3 to 0.5%. Same suppliers, same orders, the 4% stays in your pocket. Stop letting your bank quietly take a chunk of your budget every year. Search WorldFirst. Now.

Brand-polish iteration deleted / added

Stop. Real talk for everyFor every business owner sourcing from China. Your sourcing budget shrinks 4% every year and you can't see it. 500K spend? Your bank quietly kept 20,000 dollars in the exchange rate margin. You'll never find it on a statement. It's baked into the conversion. And here's the part that gets memost people miss. You're negotiating MOQ with the supplier. You're squeezing unit price. You're chasing 1% on the PI. Meanwhile your bank is taking 4% of the whole budget. Wrong fight. Wrong opponent. WorldFirst runs the same wires at 0.3 to 0.5%. Same suppliers, same orders, the 4% stays in your pocket. Stop letting your bank quietly takeWorth a look if your bank is quietly taking a chunk of your budget every year. Search WorldFirst. Now.

Skeptic test · what a Malaysian SMB might push back on

Same core math as Hormozi (4%, 20K, 500K) so inherits same defensibility profile. The 'wrong fight wrong opponent' reframe is the strongest beat and is psychologically true. Softer brand close ('worth a look') reduces ad feel. The 1% MOQ negotiation vs 4% bank margin comparison is illustrative. Main risk is still universal 4% figure. 'The 4% stays in your pocket' overstates because WorldFirst still has 0.3 to 0.5% margin so really it's 3.5% to 3.7% that stays in pocket.

  • Wrong fight wrong opponent. Ok that part true. Never thought to fight with my bank.
  • 4% on every wire? Where the number from? My bank not so bad.
  • 500K sourcing a year, that's big SME. Small importer like me, maybe 80K to 150K.
  • 4% stays in my pocket? But WorldFirst also take some right. Not free.
  • Worth a look feel less pushy than the others. But still want to see real rate.
#9 PG3 Gary Vee Opinion-led 123 words

Wrong-fight reframe

Soc4.0 Aud4.2 Sales4.2 Overall4.1 HeatMedium

Hammer/wood analogy is plain English and lands. 'Use the right tool for the job' is generic but clean.

Pure original

Stop negotiating with your supplier and start negotiating with your bank. I'm serious. You spent three weeks last quarter pushing your factory for a 2% price drop on a unit. You felt smart. Meanwhile your bank took 4% of every wire to that factory and you didn't blink. 500K of annual sourcing. 20,000 dollars in margin. Gone. Not in the supplier's pocket. In the bank's pocket. You're fighting the wrong fight. The factory is not your leak. The rail is your leak. WorldFirst sits at 0.3 to 0.5% on the same wires to the same factories. The 4% comes home. Run your numbers. If you spent more than 250K on Chinese suppliers last year, this is the highest-ROI hour you'll have this month. Search WorldFirst. Now.

Brand-polish iteration deleted / added

Stop negotiating with your supplier and start negotiatingTry negotiating with your bank instead of your supplier this quarter. I'm serious. You spent three weeks last quarter pushing your factory for a 2% price drop on a unit. You felt smart. Meanwhile your bank took 4% of every wire to that factory and you didn't blink. 500K of annual sourcing. 20,000 dollars in margin. Gone. Not in the supplier's pocket. In the bank's pocket. You're fighting the wrong fight. The factory is not your leak. The rail is your leak. WorldFirst sits at 0.3 to 0.5% on the same wires to the same factories. The 4% comes home. Run your numbers. If you spent more than 250K on Chinese suppliers last year, this is the highest-ROI hour you'll have this month. Search WorldFirst. Now.

Skeptic test · what a Malaysian SMB might push back on

The 'try negotiating with your bank instead of supplier' frame is the standout angle in the set. It connects to a real importer behaviour (over-investing time in supplier negotiation while ignoring bank margin) and is psychologically defensible. 'You're fighting the wrong fight' is opinion not claim. '250K threshold' adds specificity. Main red flag is again universal 4% and 'on the same wires to the same factories' which slightly overstates because rail differences exist. Internal logic holds.

  • Negotiating with my bank, never tried that. They got dedicated relationship manager actually.
  • 250K threshold ok that's helpful framing. I'm above that.
  • Same wires same factories on WorldFirst? Sure or not. Got different rail right.
  • 4% margin everywhere lah. CIMB charge me about 1.5%.
  • Highest ROI hour this month, bit hyperbolic but I get the point.
#10 PG4 Gary Vee Opinion-led 132 words

Truth nobody's telling you / conspiratorial

Soc4.2 Aud4.1 Sales4.0 Overall4.1 HeatMedium

'Your bank doesn't make money on the wire fee. They make money on the exchange rate.' = the kind of line a peer would quote. CTA soft — no explicit 'Search WorldFirst'.

Pure original

Here's the truth nobody's telling business owners about international wires. Your bank's biggest revenue line on your account is not your wire fee. It's your exchange rate. They quote you USD-CNY at a rate 4% worse than the real market rate. On 500K of sourcing, that's 20K a year. Compound it. Three years, 66K. Five years, over 110K. Silent. No statement entry. No annual summary. You'll never see it because it's not a fee, it's a margin. And the worst part. You can't fix this by negotiating with your supplier. They never had the money. Your bank took it on the conversion. WorldFirst doesn't play this game. 0.3 to 0.5% on the same wires. The rate you see is close to the rate banks use with each other. That's the difference. Period.

Brand-polish iteration deleted / added

Here's the truthsomething nobody's telling business owners about international wires. Your bank's biggest revenue line on your account is not your wire fee. It's your exchange rate. They quote you USD-CNY at a rate 4% worse than the real market rate. On 500K of sourcing, that's 20K a year. Compound it. Three years, 66K. Five years, over 110K. SilentQuiet. No statement entry. No annual summary. You'll never see it because it's not a fee, it's a margin. And the worst partpart most business owners miss. You can't fix this by negotiating with your supplier. They never had the money. Your bank took it on the conversion. WorldFirst doesn't play this gameis built differently. 0.3 to 0.5% on the same wires. The rate you see is close to the rate banks use with each other. That's the difference. Period.

Skeptic test · what a Malaysian SMB might push back on

'Bank's biggest revenue line on your account is your exchange rate' is bold and arguably true for SME accounts, defensible as opinion. The 5-year 110K compound is the highest-stakes claim in the script and assumes constant 4% margin plus constant 10% growth. 'They never had the money' supplier reframe is the strongest and most defensible part. 'Rate banks use with each other' is technically interbank rate which WorldFirst doesn't actually give, but 'close to' was added in iteration which softens it correctly.

  • 110K over 5 years assume too many things. Cooked numbers.
  • Biggest revenue line on my account is the rate. Damn never thought about that.
  • Close to the rate banks use with each other. Soft hedge. Means they still take a cut lah.
  • Supplier never had the money, technically true. Good reframe.
  • Who is WorldFirst? Need to research. SEA team?

Agent for P2-A8-1 is still running. Page rebuilds as each lands.

Card P2-A9Pillar P2Short-form 45sTopic 02Curiosity-led
#1 PH2 Alex Hormozi PAS 125 words

Word-for-word translation reveal

Soc4.5 Aud4.2 Sales4.3 Overall4.3 HeatMedium

Compounding maths (1.2K → 28.8K/yr → 144K/5yr) lands hard. 'A salary. A warehouse. Your kid's college fund.' = concrete stakes.

Pure original

Let me translate what your bank actually said. We'll give you a good rate. Translation. We'll give you a rate that sounds good next to the rate we'd give a tourist. 10am check. Interbank USD-CNY, the rate banks use with each other, sitting at 7.22. Bank quote on your wire. 7.05. The word good is doing 2.4 percent of heavy lifting. On a 50K supplier payment that's 1,200 dollars your bank kept and never line-itemed. Stop accepting adjectives. Start asking for the number. Ask your bank, what's your USD-CNY rate today, then open Google and compare. Half a percent off is normal. Two percent off is them charging you for the word good. WorldFirst gives you near-interbank rates with no adjective games. Search WorldFirst. Period.

Brand-polish iteration deleted / added

Let me translate what your bank actually said. We'll give you a good rate. Translation. We'll give you a rate that sounds good next to the rate we'd give a tourist. 10am check. Interbank USD-CNY, the rate banks use with each other, sitting at 7.22. Bank quote on your wire. 7.05. The word good is doing 2.4 percent of heavy lifting. On a 50K supplier payment that's 1,200 dollars your bank kept and never line-itemed. Stop accepting adjectives. Start asking for the numberStart asking for the number instead of the adjective. Ask your bank, what's your USD-CNY rate today, then open Google and compare. Half a percent off is normal. Two percent off is them charging you for the word good. WorldFirst gives you near-interbank rates with no adjective games. Search WorldFirst. Period.

Skeptic test · what a Malaysian SMB might push back on

The translation framing (good rate = good compared to tourist rate) is clever but slightly snarky and could put a banking-defensive viewer on edge. The 7.22 vs 7.05 single-moment example is consistent with PH1. 'No adjective games' on WF side is a fair brand promise but reads as marketing. 'Half a percent normal, two percent is them charging you' is again a heuristic presented as if it's universal. The core mechanism teaching is sound.

  • Tourist rate? Cmon now, business wire not same as money changer lah.
  • 1,200 on 50K, ok the math works. But once-off example or always?
  • Half a percent normal, two percent bad. Where got rule like this?
  • WorldFirst no adjective game, sure or not? Read your own marketing first.
  • What's your fee model then? Free? Hidden somewhere also?
#2 PG5 Gary Vee Opinion-led 126 words

Personal energy + verify-it-yourself

Soc4.4 Aud4.3 Sales4.2 Overall4.3 HeatLow

Call-the-bank scenario is the most relatable in the batch. 'You sleep' close is a strong payoff line.

Pure original

Real talk for any business owner who has ever heard their bank say we'll give you a good rate. I see this every week. The relationship manager calls. They use the word good. The business owner feels valued. The wire goes out. The supplier gets less than expected. And nobody connects the dots. The dots are this. Good is not a rate. It's a feeling. The actual rate, the real interbank rate that banks use with each other, you can pull up on Google in 5 seconds. Search USD to CNY. Compare. If your bank is 2 percent off, the word good cost you 2 percent of every wire you sent this year. That's real money. Stop trusting the adjective. Trust the number. WorldFirst gives you the number. Search WorldFirst. Now.

Brand-polish iteration deleted / added

Real talk for anyFor any business owner who has ever heard their bank say we'll give you a good rate. I see thisThis happens every week. The relationship manager calls. They use the word good. The business owner feels valued. The wire goes out. The supplier gets less than expected. And nobody connects the dots. The dots are this. Good is not a rate. It's a feeling. The actual rate, the real interbank rate that banks use with each other, you can pull up on Google in 5 seconds. Search USD to CNY. Compare. If your bank is 2 percent off, the word good cost you 2 percent of every wire you sent this year. That's real money. Stop trusting the adjective. Trust the numberTrust the number, not the adjective. WorldFirst gives you the number. Search WorldFirst. Now.

Skeptic test · what a Malaysian SMB might push back on

Most defensible script in the Gary Vee set. The RM-calls-you scenario is recognizable to any importer. 'Good is not a rate, it's a feeling' is clean opinion landing that doesn't claim a number. The 'every week' framing is softened to 'this happens' which removes the I-see-this overreach. Verify-yourself instruction (Google USD to CNY, 5 seconds) is concrete and free. No specific dollar amount claimed, only a 2 percent illustration. Light on numbers means light on red flag risk.

  • RM call use word good, ya this part very real. Happens to me also.
  • Good is a feeling not a rate, ok decent line.
  • 2 percent of every wire this year, big assumption on volume.
  • Google 5 seconds, fair enough. I can do this myself.
  • Who is WorldFirst? Got branch in Malaysia or only app?
#3 PH1 Alex Hormozi PAS 144 words

Phrase-decode classic PAS

Soc4.2 Aud4.0 Sales4.3 Overall4.2 HeatMedium

'This is not a fee problem. This is a hidden margin problem.' is a strong reframe line. Clean PAS rhythm intact after polish.

Pure original

Listen. When your bank tells you we'll give you a good rate, they mean good for them. Here's the math. 10am Tuesday. Real interbank USD-CNY, the rate banks use with each other, is 7.22. Your bank quotes you 7.05. That looks fine. It's not. The gap is 2.4 percent. On a 10K wire that's 240 dollars gone. On 100K it's 2,400. Same percent. Scales with every wire you send. You never see it on a statement because it's baked into the rate itself. Banks compete on the word good. You should compete on the actual number. Stop. Open XE.com or just Google USD to CNY before you confirm the wire. If your quote is more than half a percent off, you're paying a silent margin. Period. WorldFirst quotes near the interbank rate so the number you see is the number you pay. Search WorldFirst. Period.

Brand-polish iteration deleted / added

Listen. When your bank tells you we'll give you a good rate, they mean good for them. Here's the math. 10am Tuesday. Real interbank USD-CNY, the rate banks use with each other, is 7.22. Your bank quotes you 7.05. That looks fine. It's not. The gap is 2.4 percent. On a 10K wire that's 240 dollars gone. On 100K it's 2,400. Same percent. Scales with every wire you send. You never see it on a statement because it's baked into the rate itself. Banks compete on the word good. You should compete on the actual number. Stop. Open XE.com or just Google USD to CNY before you confirm the wire. If your quote is more than half a percent off, you're paying a silent margin. Period. WorldFirst quotes near the interbank rate so the number you see is the number you pay. Worth a look. Search WorldFirst. Period.

Skeptic test · what a Malaysian SMB might push back on

Core teaching (the word 'good' is unverifiable until compared to interbank) is genuinely useful and defensible. The specific 7.22 vs 7.05 example is presented as if it's a single observed moment, which is fair as illustration. Main red flag is the universal-feeling 2.4 percent presented as a typical case, when actual spreads vary by bank, currency pair, wire size, and tier. 'Number you see is the number you pay' on WF side slightly overstates because CN-side receiving fees can still apply. The 'half a percent off' threshold is a reasonable heuristic but is presented as a hard rule.

  • 7.22 vs 7.05? One snapshot only what. CNY rate move every minute lah.
  • Half a percent threshold, who set this rule? Sound made up.
  • My Maybank quote not so bad. Maybe US bank different.
  • Who is WorldFirst? Got Malaysia license or not?
  • Sound like ad. Show me real screenshot side by side la.
#4 PH3 Alex Hormozi PAS 122 words

Accusation around the marketing word

Soc4.4 Aud3.9 Sales4.3 Overall4.2 HeatHigh

Accusation opener is visceral. 'You signed off on it' might still feel slightly attacking — close to the line for SEA register.

Pure original

The word good in we'll give you a good rate is the most expensive word in banking. Here's why. Tuesday 10am. Interbank USD-CNY, the rate banks use with each other, is 7.22. Your bank's good rate. 7.05. That gap is 2.4 percent. On a 10K wire, 240 dollars. 100K, 2,400. Now do the math across a year of supplier wires. The word good cost you a hire. The word good cost you your Q3 inventory buffer. The word good cost you the margin you needed to compete on price. Banks weaponize the word good because business owners don't check. Stop letting an adjective decide your P&L. Open XE.com. Compare. Demand the number not the word. WorldFirst quotes near-interbank with the rate visible before you confirm. Search WorldFirst. Period.

Brand-polish iteration deleted / added

The word good in we'll give you a good rate is the most expensive word in banking. Here's why. Tuesday 10am. Interbank USD-CNY, the rate banks use with each other, is 7.22. Your bank's good rate. 7.05. That gap is 2.4 percent. On a 10K wire, 240 dollars. 100K, 2,400. Now do the math across a year of supplier wires. The word good cost you a hire. The word good cost you your Q3 inventory buffer. The word good cost you the margin you needed to compete on price. Banks weaponizelean on the word good because business owners don't check. Stop letting an adjective decide your P&L. Open XE.com. Compare. DemandAsk for the number not the word. WorldFirst quotes near-interbank with the rate visible before you confirm. Search WorldFirst. Period.

Skeptic test · what a Malaysian SMB might push back on

The compounding consequences (cost you a hire, your Q3 inventory buffer, your competing margin) are dramatic extrapolations from a single 2.4 percent figure. Most SMBs will see this as stretched. 'Banks lean on the word good because business owners don't check' is slightly accusatory of the audience. The 7.22 vs 7.05 example is fine, but stacking it into 'a year of supplier wires' without specifying volume is the same compounding risk as PH2 in the reference set. Most defensible part is the verify-yourself instruction.

  • Cost me a hire? Bro you don't know my volume. Maybe I do 2 wires a year.
  • Business owners don't check, ok so you call us lazy? Thanks.
  • Where 2.4% come from consistently? My bank not so bad.
  • Q3 inventory buffer? Sound like consultant talk. Just show real rate la.
  • Who is WorldFirst? Need license check first.
#5 PH5 Alex Hormozi PAS 138 words

Two-column reveal + verify-yourself

Soc4.3 Aud4.1 Sales4.2 Overall4.2 HeatLow

1-2-3 structural reveal works well. Listing what you saw vs didn't see is clean teaching.

Pure original

If your relationship manager said we'll give you a good rate, you need this. Here's the test. Two columns. Left column. What the bank quoted you. Say 7.05 USD to CNY on a wire to your supplier. Right column. What the rate actually was at that minute. Real interbank, the rate banks use with each other. 7.22. The gap is 2.4 percent. On 10K, you paid 240 hidden. On 50K, 1,200 hidden. On 100K, 2,400 hidden. None of it shows up as a fee. You don't need a treasury team. You need one browser tab. XE.com or Google USD to CNY. Compare before you confirm. If your bank's rate is more than half a percent off the real one, the word good is doing the lying. WorldFirst quotes near-interbank with the live rate shown before confirmation. Search WorldFirst. Period.

Brand-polish iteration deleted / added

If your relationship manager said we'll give you a good rate, you need this. Here's the test. Two columns. Left column. What the bank quoted you. Say 7.05 USD to CNY on a wire to your supplier. Right column. What the rate actually was at that minute. Real interbank, the rate banks use with each other. 7.22. The gap is 2.4 percent. On 10K, you paid 240 hidden. On 50K, 1,200 hidden. On 100K, 2,400 hidden. None of it shows up as a fee. You don't need a treasury team. You need one browser tab. XE.com or Google USD to CNY. Compare before you confirm. If your bank's rate is more than half a percent off the real one, the word good is doing the lyingwork. WorldFirst quotes near-interbank with the live rate shown before confirmation. Search WorldFirst. Period.

Skeptic test · what a Malaysian SMB might push back on

The two-column test is the cleanest teaching device in the Hormozi set. Concrete amounts at 10K, 50K, 100K let the viewer find their own size. 'One browser tab' is honest minimum-friction framing. 'Half a percent off' threshold is again a heuristic but here it's labeled as rule of thumb, not law. 'The word good is doing the work' is softer than 'doing the lying' (which was the original) and lands without insulting bank or viewer. Most defensible script in the Hormozi set.

  • Two column test ok I can do this. Practical advice for once.
  • Half percent threshold still feel made up but at least sound reasonable.
  • Live rate shown before confirmation, sure WorldFirst do this?
  • Who is WorldFirst? Never hear before in KL.
  • Compare before confirm, ok useful tip even if I don't switch.
#6 PG2 Gary Vee Opinion-led 131 words

I'll say it opinion-as-truth

Soc4.3 Aud4.1 Sales4.2 Overall4.2 HeatMedium

'Your bank is the most expensive part of your supply chain' is the sharpest single line in this batch. Tight at 97 words.

Pure original

I'll say it. Good rate is the most successful piece of marketing the banks ever ran. Because good is comparative and they get to pick what it's compared to. You send a wire to your supplier in China. Bank says good rate. You feel taken care of. Meanwhile the real interbank rate, the rate banks use with each other, is sitting 2 to 3 percent better than what they just quoted you. On 50K, that's a thousand dollars or more, gone in one click. You'll never see it because the margin lives inside the rate, not on the statement. The fix is so simple. Google USD to CNY before you confirm. If you're more than half a percent off, you're paying for the word good. WorldFirst publishes rates near interbank. Numbers, not adjectives. Search WorldFirst.

Brand-polish iteration deleted / added

I'll say it. Good rate is the most successful piece of marketing the banks ever ran. Because good is comparative and they get to pick what it's compared to. You send a wire to your supplier in China. Bank says good rate. You feel taken care of. Meanwhile the real interbank rate, the rate banks use with each other, is sitting 2 to 3 percent better than what they just quoted you. On 50K, that's a thousand dollars or more, gone in one click. You'll never see it because the margin lives inside the rate, not on the statement. The fix is so simple. Google USD to CNY before you confirm. If you're more than half a percent off, you're paying for the word good. WorldFirst publishes rates near interbank. Numbers, not adjectives. Search WorldFirst.

Skeptic test · what a Malaysian SMB might push back on

'Most successful piece of marketing the banks ever ran' is opinion framed as opinion, defensible Gary Vee voice. The '2 to 3 percent' range is more honest than a single 2.4 percent figure and shows it varies. 'Margin lives inside the rate, not on the statement' is accurate. 'Half a percent off' threshold again presented without source. WF claim 'numbers not adjectives' is clean brand positioning that doesn't overstate function.

  • Most successful marketing, fair opinion. Bit dramatic but ok.
  • 2 to 3 percent range, better than fixed number. But still got source?
  • Half percent threshold rule, where from? Sound arbitrary.
  • Margin in rate not statement, ya this part true actually.
  • Who is WorldFirst? Got license in Malaysia or just online thing?
#7 PH4 Alex Hormozi PAS 131 words

Mechanism reveal, no accusation

Soc4.1 Aud4.1 Sales4.2 Overall4.1 HeatLow

'The fee is not the fee' = sharp reframe. 'Banks make billions' is a broad claim that could draw pushback.

Pure original

Here's what most business owners don't realize about the phrase good rate. Good is not a rate. It's a relative word. Good compared to what. Here's the answer your bank hopes you never ask. Tuesday 10am. Real interbank USD-CNY, the rate banks use with each other, is 7.22. Your bank's good rate. 7.05. The gap is 2.4 percent. On every wire. Buried in the exchange rate itself, never a line item on the statement. So when the bank says good, they mean better than what they could legally charge a tourist. Not better than the real cost. Better than the worst case. The fix is one tab. XE.com. Google. Wise mid-market quote. All free. All real-time. Compare before you confirm. WorldFirst publishes rates near interbank so good actually means good. Search WorldFirst. Period.

Brand-polish iteration deleted / added

Here's what most business owners don't realize about the phrase good rate. Good is not a rate. It's a relative word. Good compared to what. Here's the answer your bank hopes you never ask. Tuesday 10am. Real interbank USD-CNY, the rate banks use with each other, is 7.22. Your bank's good rate. 7.05. The gap is 2.4 percent. On every wire. Buried in the exchange rate itself, never a line item on the statement. So when the bank says good, they mean better than what they could legally charge a tourist. Not better than the real cost. Better than the worst case. The fix is one tab. XE.com. Google. Wise mid-market quote. All free. All real-time. Compare before you confirm. WorldFirst publishes rates near interbank so good actually means good. Search WorldFirst. Period.

Skeptic test · what a Malaysian SMB might push back on

Cleanest of the Hormozi set. The 'good compared to what' question is genuinely useful pedagogy. The 7.22 vs 7.05 example is bounded as illustration not universal claim. Spelling out interbank ('the rate banks use with each other') and listing free verification tools (XE.com, Google, Wise mid-market) is concrete and actionable. 'Better than what they could charge a tourist' is mild snark but defensible because banks do tier rates. Main red flag is still that 2.4 percent reads as typical when it's just one example.

  • Good compared to what, ya this question actually useful.
  • XE Google Wise, ok concrete. I can verify. Fair enough.
  • Tourist rate comparison, my BNM regulated bank not the same lah.
  • Still want to know who WorldFirst is. License? Parent company?
  • 2.4% on this one example. What about other days when bank rate is closer?
#8 PG1 Gary Vee Opinion-led 103 words

Opinion-led phrase callout

Soc4.0 Aud4.1 Sales4.2 Overall4.1 HeatMedium

Lost some of the original Gary Vee punch after polish. Still works but feels closer to baseline.

Pure original

Stop. Real talk for any business owner sending money to Chinese suppliers. When your bank says we'll give you a good rate, that is marketing. It is not a number. Here's what good actually meant on a real wire last Tuesday. Real interbank USD-CNY, the rate banks use with each other, was 7.22. The bank quoted my friend 7.05 on a 50K wire. The word good cost him 1,200 dollars. Buried in the rate. No line item. Listen. Adjectives are not rates. Numbers are rates. Next time, ask for the number. Compare it to Google. WorldFirst gives you near-interbank quotes with the actual number shown. Search WorldFirst. Now.

Here's what good actually meant on a real wire last Tuesday. Real interbank USD-CNY, the rate banks use with each other, was 7.22. The bank quoted my friend 7.05 on a 50K wire. The word good cost him 1,200 dollars. Buried in the rate. No line item. Listen. Adjectives are not rates. Numbers are rates. Next time, ask for the number. Compare it to Google. WorldFirst gives you near-interbank quotes with the actual number shown. Search WorldFirst. Now.

Skeptic test · what a Malaysian SMB might push back on

'Adjectives are not rates' is clean Gary Vee opinion landing. The single 7.22 vs 7.05 example with the friend story personalizes it. 'No line item' is technically accurate and a legitimate teaching point. Main risk is the friend anecdote is unverifiable, and 'good cost him 1,200 dollars' is one example presented to imply norm. WF claim 'actual number shown' is a brand promise that a skeptic will want to test.

  • Your friend, sure or just a made up example?
  • Adjective not rate, fine. But all banks use marketing words lah, normal.
  • 1,200 on 50K once, but my bank maybe charge 500. Different.
  • Who is WorldFirst? Need to research first.
  • Sound like ad. Got real customer testimonial from SE Asia SME?
#9 PG3 Gary Vee Opinion-led 110 words

Stop accepting adjectives reframe

Soc4.0 Aud4.2 Sales4.2 Overall4.1 HeatMedium

Hammer/wood analogy is plain English and lands. 'Use the right tool for the job' is generic but clean.

Pure original

Stop accepting adjectives from your bank. I'm serious. Good rate. Best rate. Preferred rate. Special rate. None of those are numbers. None of those tell you what your supplier actually receives in China. Here's what one looked like last week. Interbank USD-CNY, the rate banks use with each other, 7.22. Bank's good rate to a business owner I know, 7.05. Gap, 2.4 percent. On his 80K wire, that's almost 2K vanishing into the rate. You wouldn't sign a contract with the word good as a price. Don't accept it on a wire either. Ask for the number. Compare to Google. WorldFirst quotes near-interbank with the number shown before you confirm. Search WorldFirst. Now.

Brand-polish iteration deleted / added

Stop accepting adjectives from your bank. I'm seriousYour bank is selling you adjectives. Good rate. Best rate. Preferred rate. Special rate. None of those are numbers. None of those tell you what your supplier actually receives in China. Here's what one looked like last week. Interbank USD-CNY, the rate banks use with each other, 7.22. Bank's good rate to a business owner I know, 7.05. Gap, 2.4 percent. On his 80K wire, that's almost 2K vanishing into the rate. You wouldn't sign a contract with the word good as a price. Don't accept it on a wire either. Ask for the number. Compare to Google. WorldFirst quotes near-interbank with the number shown before you confirm. Search WorldFirst. Now.

Skeptic test · what a Malaysian SMB might push back on

'Selling you adjectives' as opener is clean reframe and lands the phrase-decode angle. Listing 'good rate, best rate, preferred rate, special rate' is genuine and useful pattern recognition. The 80K example differs from prior 50K which adds variety but the 2.4 percent is again consistent across scripts which a binge-watcher could question. Wouldn't sign a contract with adjective as price is a strong analogy that a skeptic might accept.

  • Best rate preferred rate special rate, ya banks do use these. Pattern is real.
  • 80K wire example, who you know that sends 80K? Big SME already.
  • Contract with adjective as price analogy, ok this one land.
  • 2.4% again, every script same number. You only got one data point?
  • Show me WorldFirst's actual rate vs my Maybank. Side by side.
#10 PG4 Gary Vee Opinion-led 115 words

Truth nobody tells / conspiratorial

Soc4.2 Aud4.1 Sales4.0 Overall4.1 HeatMedium

'Your bank doesn't make money on the wire fee. They make money on the exchange rate.' = the kind of line a peer would quote. CTA soft — no explicit 'Search WorldFirst'.

Pure original

Here's the truth nobody is telling business owners about the phrase good rate. It's a trick of comparison. Banks compare your rate to a worse rate they could have given you and call it good. They don't compare it to the real interbank rate, the rate banks use with each other. On a typical day, that gap is 2 percent. Sometimes more. On 50K, that's 1,000 to 1,500 dollars. Per wire. Never on the statement. The word good is doing all the work. Pull up Google. Search USD to CNY. Look at the number. Then look at your bank's quote. The gap is what good was hiding. WorldFirst quotes near interbank. Adjective free. Search WorldFirst.

Brand-polish iteration deleted / added

Here's the truthsomething nobody is telling business owners about the phrase good rate. It's a trick of comparison. Banks compare your rate to a worse rate they could have given you and call it good. They don't compare it to the real interbank rate, the rate banks use with each other. On a typical day, that gap is 2 percent. Sometimes more. On 50K, that's 1,000 to 1,500 dollars. Per wire. Never on the statement. The word good is doing all the work. Pull up Google. Search USD to CNY. Look at the number. Then look at your bank's quote. The gap is what good was hiding. WorldFirst quotes near interbank. Adjective freeNumbers, not adjectives. Search WorldFirst.

Skeptic test · what a Malaysian SMB might push back on

'Trick of comparison' is sharp framing. The teaching that banks compare to worse-internal-rate not real interbank is genuinely useful and largely accurate. '2 percent typical, sometimes more' is honest range. '1,000 to 1,500 dollars per wire' on 50K aligns with the 2-3 percent range, internally consistent. 'Never on the statement' is technically accurate. Main risk is the 'trick' framing is mildly accusatory and a skeptic may push back.

  • Trick of comparison, ok this actually a good way to frame it.
  • 2% typical, source please? Or your opinion only?
  • 1000 to 1500 on 50K. Math checks out. But every bank like this?
  • Never on statement, ya FX margin baked in rate. This part true.
  • Who is WorldFirst? Need license proof before I move money.
